title: Hierarchical: Slow Meta, Fast Base

runtime.expected_outcome: Meta-level switches contexts on a slow schedule; base agent executes fast tactical steps within each context. The two timescales decouple cleanly.

audiences.kid: The boss checks in occasionally; the worker moves a lot in between.
audiences.real: Hierarchical active inference naturally separates timescales -- meta runs slow (strategic), base runs fast (tactical). Using steps_in_context to gate context switches.
audiences.equation: Switch context when steps_in_context >= k, or when a meta-level prediction error exceeds a threshold.
audiences.derivation: Timescale separation is emergent from the hierarchical generative model: slower dynamics at higher levels reflect slower-varying environmental statistics (Parr et al. 2022, Ch 7).

orchestrate.objective: Run a 2-level hierarchical agent with slow meta switching and fast base action.
orchestrate.context: hierarchical_maze 11x11; meta switches every 5 base steps.

credits: Parr, Pezzulo & Friston (2022), Active Inference, MIT Press (CC BY-NC-ND), Ch 7.
